title: If Rei and Shinji become aware of EVAs secret
display_title: If Rei and Shinji become aware of EVAs secret
wikitext:
[]: If Rei and Shinji become aware of EVA's secret...             Quote Post by DAVEvangelion » Mon Dec 21, 2009 10:10 am "If Rei and Shinji become aware of EVA's secret, they won't forgive us." Ritsuko says this to Gendo at the end of episode 16. Just out of curiosity, what secret specifically is Ritsuko referring to? Why wouldn't Shinji and Rei forgive them? Why isn't Asuka mentioned? This little bit of dialogue always bugged me.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 21, 2009 12:17 pm My guess is it's something to do with Yui's soul being in Eva-01 since Rei=Yui's DNA and Shinji=Yui's son. Rei at least seems aware the Eva have souls and that Rei 1 is in Eva-00, but whether or not she knows whose souls are in the other Eva, it's hard to tell.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Mon Dec 21, 2009 12:58 pm There was a thread about this but I can't find it. If anyone knows where it is, please lead it to this thread. Also, I changed the title to something less vague.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Dec 21, 2009 2:37 pm Shinji would never forgive them if he new what was really going on with EVA, but Rei on the other hand probably already knows almost everything anyway so Ritsuko’s warning doesn’t really apply to her.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Dec 21, 2009 3:19 pm Asuka is a heartless she-bitch from the depths of Hell, whom cares for nothing except for gaining apocalyptic power. Things like "morality" and "the sanctity of life" hold no meaning for one such as her.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 21, 2009 3:38 pm Is the only other thread I found. http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2409 As far as knowing one way or the other: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3972 (although it's a Magma Diver thread, it goes into Ritsuko's line: "If Rei or Shinji were to find out the Eva's secrets, they'd never forgive us")

[]: Re: If Rei and Shinji become aware of EVA's secret...             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Dec 21, 2009 5:57 pm DAVEvangelion wrote: ...what secret specifically is Ritsuko referring to?

[]:             Quote Post by DAVEvangelion » Mon Dec 21, 2009 7:15 pm ZapX wrote: There was a thread about this but I can't find it. If anyone knows where it is, please lead it to this thread. Also, I changed the title to something less vague.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Dec 21, 2009 7:27 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Asuka is a heartless she-bitch from the depths of Hell, whom cares for nothing except for gaining apocalyptic power. Things like "morality" and "the sanctity of life" hold no meaning for one such as her.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Tue Dec 22, 2009 5:43 am AuraTwilight wrote: Asuka is a heartless she-bitch from the depths of Hell, whom cares for nothing except for gaining apocalyptic power. Things like "morality" and "the sanctity of life" hold no meaning for one such as her.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Dec 22, 2009 2:13 pm Pfft, you wish you were that influential, don't you? I've always disliked Asuka. I just felt like being over the top.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Dec 22, 2009 3:59 pm There is one large problem with Ritsuko's line and that is what Rei does not know about the Evas. She probably already knows about "the me inside the Eva", i.e. Rei I, so exactly what the secret is would have to be something other than the general theory of souls in Evas. My own feeling is that the "secret" has to do with Unit-01 and not the other Evas specifically. Ritsuko's referally to Rei might be related to Rei's earlier "lapse" in the same episode when Unit-01 is swallowed by Leliel. Rei becomes unusually agitated but her words reveal a little something more than concern for Shinji Episode #16 wrote: Rei : Wait! Unit 01 and Ikari are still in there.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaz » Wed Dec 23, 2009 9:19 am I allways thought she was refering to the fact that the Evas are actually living creatures and not gigantic robots.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 23, 2009 10:04 am Kaz wrote: I allways thought she was refering to the fact that the Evas are actually living creatures and not gigantic robots.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Tue Jun 01, 2010 1:17 am The theory is possible, but if you remember the whole confrontation with Asuka, Shinji appears to be the main focus os Rei's concern. Of course Unit 1 is important, but not much else is know to Rei, at least that is implied throughout the series. Rei doesn't really show much concern for Unit 1 in the series, and if she does it's mainly because of it's necessity for defeating angels in the beginning. As her relationship with Shinji deepens she does more and more things that are out of character: Speaks out against orders, confronts Asuka, thanks him (only one she's ever thanked), and ultimately dies for him. The secret Ritsuko is referring to is about Rei and Shinji's Eva souls. Shinji wouldn't forgive them for what happened to his mother (regardless if it was her choice or not). Eva 00 is up for controversy, but it is highly believed that half of Rei I's soul is in it. Rei II is still the same person but without those memories of her death, so if she were to find out what happened to her first incarnation then she'd be mad at Gendo because he was the one who indirectly caused her death. It can be assumed that Gendo sent Rei I to tell Naoko Akagi of Gendo's rejection of her and he knew her likeness to Yui would cause the woman to do something rash (aka kill the child). The whole Unit 00 going after Gendo in it's first activation thing alludes to possible anger Rei I would feel for being betrayed. This can be seen after Rei III (the now complete soul of Rei) begins to break Gendo's glasses and ultimately bertays him for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Jun 01, 2010 10:27 am Hiten777 wrote: Rei II is still the same person but without those memories of her death, so if she were to find out what happened to her first incarnation then she'd be mad at Gendo because he was the one who indirectly caused her death. It can be assumed that Gendo sent Rei I to tell Naoko Akagi of Gendo's rejection of her and he knew her likeness to Yui would cause the woman to do something rash (aka kill the child). The whole Unit 00 going after Gendo in it's first activation thing alludes to possible anger Rei I would feel for being betrayed. This can be seen after Rei III (the now complete soul of Rei) begins to break Gendo's glasses and ultimately bertays him for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Tue Jun 01, 2010 11:57 pm I never said it was fact but most likely a factor if you take into account the things that happen as a whole in the show. Who is the soul inside Eva 00? Could Eva 00 be able to operate without a soul? Most likely not since it's only the prototype. So with that out in the open, there must be something in there that relates to Rei, because, as with Unit 01, if the soul was someone who was unrelated to Rei then she wouldn't be able to operate it at all because of the synch rejection. 1) Rei I's soul would be the most likely candidate for a possible synch with Rei's next incarnation since Rei biologically doesn't have a mother. A mother's soul is necessary to synch with the child when applied to the earlier Eva productions. 2) Gendo is concerned for Rei II because he is concerned for her role as a pilot and the main means to get Yui back. The manga makes a good point when Rei states that he doesn't really see her for herself but as a representation of someone else (Yui). He already knows what will happen in the future because of the Dead Sea Scrolls, so more than likely he knows that Rei will eventually have to be revived. 3) True if he had wanted Naoko dead he could've easily told someone to do it secretly. But the situation is what made it her own fault for her demise. Think about it, how would he get Ritsuko's trust if she, someone who knows almost all of the secrets behind the scenes, were to know, if by digging or being told, that her mother was murdered by the person she loved. Ritsuko's abilities are necessary to win against the angels, and even thought her relationship with Naoko was disfunctional she still had respect and love for her. In EoE she hoped to end it all for both her and her mother by killing Gendo. 4) Naoko's love interest in Gendo also annoyed him to no end. More like she was a something on the side who was getting a little too serious in his opinion. What would happen if she found out from his own mouth that he would never love her like she wanted? She created the Magi and whatnot, she basically can destroy everything that they need if she really wanted to.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Jun 02, 2010 10:57 am ^@Hiten777: I'm not completely sure if this is what you mean. Are you saying that Gendo was killing two birds with one stone by getting rid of Naoko and providing himself with a dead Rei to use as Unit-00's soul? Once again, such a roundabout method would not be neccessary. They ended up splitting Rei 1's soul between Rei 2 and Unit-00. They could have removed part of her soul and done the same thing without killing her just as they did when only part of Kyoko's soul was put into Unit-02. Also, Gendo could have easilly knocked off Naoko and made it look like an accident or suicide without trying to induce a murder/suicide with such a fantastic scheme. I also don't buy his motive for killing her at all. Since when is being clingy and annoying a capital crime? Messy deaths cause unwanted attention. Gendo isn't going to assassinate anyone unless it's vital for his plans. Watch the "Gendo to the Rescue" scene from Ep. 05 again. It's plain from the way it's portrayed that he is acting out of concern for Rei, not merely attempting pilot preservation. Yes, ultimately he plans to use Rei as a tool for uniting with Yui, but in the meantime she's more dear to him than anyone else but Yui. There's nothing in the way the Gendo/Naoko/Rei 1 business is presented that in any way suggests that Gendo set up Naoko, or intended her death at all.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 02, 2010 2:46 pm Naoko isn't Rei's mother, so it wouldn't work. If Rei/Naoko works, then any mother should work with any pilot. Yawn.

[]:             Quote Post by IkariRei » Wed Jun 02, 2010 3:09 pm Hiten777 wrote: Rei II is still the same person but without those memories of her death
